search_text: Deep Sequential Learning for Cervical Spine Fracture Detection on Computed Tomography Imaging 计算机断层扫描成像中颈椎骨折检测的深度序列学习 Fractures of the cervical spine are a medical emergency and may lead to permanent paralysis and even death. Accurate diagnosis in patients with suspected fractures by computed tomography (CT) is critical to patient management. In this paper, we propose a deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) with a bidirectional long-short term memory (BLSTM) layer for the automated detection of cervical spine fractures in CT axial images. We used an annotated dataset of 3,666 CT scans (729 positive and 2,937 negative cases) to train and validate the model. The validation results show a classification accuracy of 70.92% and 79.18% on the balanced (104 positive and 104 negative cases) and imbalanced (104 positive and 419 negative cases) test datasets, respectively. 颈椎骨折是一种医疗紧急情况，可能导致永久性瘫痪甚至死亡。通过计算机断层扫描 (CT) 对疑似骨折患者进行准确诊断对于患者管理至关重要。在本文中，我们提出了一种具有双向长期短期记忆（BLSTM）层的深度卷积神经网络（DCNN），用于自动检测 CT 轴向图像中的颈椎骨折。我们使用包含 3,666 个 CT 扫描（729 个阳性病例和 2,937 个阴性病例）的带注释数据集来训练和验证模型。验证结果显示，平衡（104 个正例和 104 个负例）和不平衡（104 个正例和 419 个负例）测试数据集的分类准确率分别为 70.92% 和 79.18%。 本文提出一种结合双向长短期记忆网络（BLSTM）的深度卷积神经网络（DCNN），用于自动检测CT影像中的颈椎骨折。 Hojjat Salehinejad,Edward Ho,Hui-Ming Lin,Priscila Crivellaro,Oleksandra Samorodova,Monica Tafur Arciniegas,Zamir Merali,Suradech Suthiphosuwan,Aditya Bharatha,Kristen Yeom,Muhammad Mamdani,Jefferson Wilson,Errol Colak eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

title: Distributed Node-Specific Block-Diagonal LCMV Beamforming in Wireless Acoustic Sensor Networks
title_zh: 无线声学传感器网络中分布式节点特定块对角 LCMV 波束形成
comment: A novel distributed node-specific LCMV beamforming in wireless acoustic sensor networks is proposed in this paper
comments: A novel distributed node-specific LCMV beamforming in wireless acoustic sensor networks is proposed in this paper
abstract: This paper derives the analytical solution of a novel distributed node-specific block-diagonal linearly constrained minimum variance beamformer from the centralized linearly constrained minimum variance (LCMV) beamformer when considering that the noise covariance matrix is block-diagonal. To further reduce the computational complexity of the proposed beamformer, the ShermanMorrison-Woodbury formula is introduced to compute the inversion of noise sample covariance matrix. By doing so, the exchanged signals can be computed with lower dimensions between nodes, where the optimal LCMV beamformer is still available at each node as if each node is to transmit its all raw sensor signal observations. The proposed beamformer is fully distributable without imposing restrictions on the underlying network topology or scaling computational complexity, i.e., there is no increase in the per-node complexity when new nodes are added to the networks. Compared with state-of-the-art distributed node-specific algorithms that are often time-recursive, the proposed beamformer exactly solves the LCMV beamformer optimally frame by frame, which has much lower computational complexity and is more robust to acoustic transfer function estimation error and voice activity detector error. Numerous experimental results are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed beamformer.
summary: This paper derives the analytical solution of a novel distributed node-specific block-diagonal linearly constrained minimum variance beamformer from the centralized linearly constrained minimum variance (LCMV) beamformer when considering that the noise covariance matrix is block-diagonal. To further reduce the computational complexity of the proposed beamformer, the ShermanMorrison-Woodbury formula is introduced to compute the inversion of noise sample covariance matrix. By doing so, the exchanged signals can be computed with lower dimensions between nodes, where the optimal LCMV beamformer is still available at each node as if each node is to transmit its all raw sensor signal observations. The proposed beamformer is fully distributable without imposing restrictions on the underlying network topology or scaling computational complexity, i.e., there is no increase in the per-node complexity when new nodes are added to the networks. Compared with state-of-the-art distributed node-specific algorithms that are often time-recursive, the proposed beamformer exactly solves the LCMV beamformer optimally frame by frame, which has much lower computational complexity and is more robust to acoustic transfer function estimation error and voice activity detector error. Numerous experimental results are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed beamformer.
abstract_zh: 考虑到噪声协方差矩阵是块对角矩阵，本文从集中式线性约束最小方差（LCMV）波束形成器推导了一种新型分布式节点特定块对角线性约束最小方差波束形成器的解析解。为了进一步降低所提出的波束形成器的计算复杂度，引入ShermanMorrison-Woodbury公式来计算噪声样本协方差矩阵的逆。通过这样做，可以在节点之间以较低维度计算交换的信号，其中最佳 LCMV 波束形成器在每个节点仍然可用，就好像每个节点要传输其所有原始传感器信号观测结果一样。所提出的波束形成器是完全可分布式的，无需对底层网络拓扑或缩放计算复杂性施加限制，即，当新节点添加到网络时，每个节点的复杂性不会增加。与通常是时间递归的最先进的分布式节点特定算法相比，所提出的波束形成器精确地逐帧求解 LCMV 波束形成器，其计算复杂度低得多，并且对声学传递函数估计误差和语音活动检测器误差更鲁棒。大量的实验结果验证了所提出的波束形成器的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种适用于无线声学传感器网络的分布式节点特定块对角线LCMV波束成形方法，通过降低计算复杂度并保持最优性能。
AI.motivation: 现有分布式波束成形算法存在计算复杂度高、对网络拓扑依赖性强且鲁棒性不足的问题，需设计一种高效且可扩展的解决方案。
AI.method: 基于集中式LCMV波束成形器，结合块对角线噪声协方差矩阵特性，引入Sherman-Morrison-Woodbury公式简化矩阵求逆，并通过节点间低维信号交换实现分布式计算。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在计算复杂度、抗声学传输函数估计误差和语音活动检测误差方面优于现有算法，且具有良好的可扩展性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的块对角线LCMV波束成形器在无线声学传感器网络中实现了高效、鲁棒且拓扑无关的分布式波束成形。

authors_text: Ting Liu,Jennifer J. Sun,Long Zhao,Jiaping Zhao,Liangzhe Yuan,Yuxiao Wang,Liang-Chieh Chen,Florian Schroff,Hartwig Adam
title: View-Invariant, Occlusion-Robust Probabilistic Embedding for Human Pose
title_zh: 用于人体姿势的视图不变、遮挡稳健的概率嵌入
comment: Accepted to International Journal of Computer Vision (IJCV). Code is available at https://github.com/google-research/google-research/tree/master/poem. Video synchronization results are available at https://drive.google.com/corp/drive/folders/1nhPuEcX4Lhe6iK3nv84cvSCov2eJ52Xy. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1912.01001
comments: Accepted to International Journal of Computer Vision (IJCV). Code is available at https://github.com/google-research/google-research/tree/master/poem. Video synchronization results are available at https://drive.google.com/corp/drive/folders/1nhPuEcX4Lhe6iK3nv84cvSCov2eJ52Xy. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1912.01001
abstract: Recognition of human poses and actions is crucial for autonomous systems to interact smoothly with people. However, cameras generally capture human poses in 2D as images and videos, which can have significant appearance variations across viewpoints that make the recognition tasks challenging. To address this, we explore recognizing similarity in 3D human body poses from 2D information, which has not been well-studied in existing works. Here, we propose an approach to learning a compact view-invariant embedding space from 2D body joint keypoints, without explicitly predicting 3D poses. Input ambiguities of 2D poses from projection and occlusion are difficult to represent through a deterministic mapping, and therefore we adopt a probabilistic formulation for our embedding space. Experimental results show that our embedding model achieves higher accuracy when retrieving similar poses across different camera views, in comparison with 3D pose estimation models. We also show that by training a simple temporal embedding model, we achieve superior performance on pose sequence retrieval and largely reduce the embedding dimension from stacking frame-based embeddings for efficient large-scale retrieval. Furthermore, in order to enable our embeddings to work with partially visible input, we further investigate different keypoint occlusion augmentation strategies during training. We demonstrate that these occlusion augmentations significantly improve retrieval performance on partial 2D input poses. Results on action recognition and video alignment demonstrate that using our embeddings without any additional training achieves competitive performance relative to other models specifically trained for each task.
summary: Recognition of human poses and actions is crucial for autonomous systems to interact smoothly with people. However, cameras generally capture human poses in 2D as images and videos, which can have significant appearance variations across viewpoints that make the recognition tasks challenging. To address this, we explore recognizing similarity in 3D human body poses from 2D information, which has not been well-studied in existing works. Here, we propose an approach to learning a compact view-invariant embedding space from 2D body joint keypoints, without explicitly predicting 3D poses. Input ambiguities of 2D poses from projection and occlusion are difficult to represent through a deterministic mapping, and therefore we adopt a probabilistic formulation for our embedding space. Experimental results show that our embedding model achieves higher accuracy when retrieving similar poses across different camera views, in comparison with 3D pose estimation models. We also show that by training a simple temporal embedding model, we achieve superior performance on pose sequence retrieval and largely reduce the embedding dimension from stacking frame-based embeddings for efficient large-scale retrieval. Furthermore, in order to enable our embeddings to work with partially visible input, we further investigate different keypoint occlusion augmentation strategies during training. We demonstrate that these occlusion augmentations significantly improve retrieval performance on partial 2D input poses. Results on action recognition and video alignment demonstrate that using our embeddings without any additional training achieves competitive performance relative to other models specifically trained for each task.
abstract_zh: 识别人类姿势和动作对于自主系统与人类顺利交互至关重要。然而，相机通常以图像和视频的形式捕捉 2D 人体姿势，这些姿势在不同视点之间可能会有显着的外观变化，这使得识别任务具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们探索从 2D 信息中识别 3D 人体姿势的相似性，这在现有的工作中尚未得到充分研究。在这里，我们提出了一种从 2D 身体关节关键点学习紧凑的视图不变嵌入空间的方法，而无需显式预测 3D 姿势。来自投影和遮挡的 2D 姿势的输入模糊性很难通过确定性映射来表示，因此我们对嵌入空间采用概率公式。实验结果表明，与 3D 姿态估计模型相比，我们的嵌入模型在跨不同相机视图检索相似姿态时实现了更高的准确度。我们还表明，通过训练一个简单的时间嵌入模型，我们在姿势序列检索方面取得了优异的性能，并大大减少了基于堆叠帧的嵌入的嵌入维度，以实现高效的大规模检索。此外，为了使我们的嵌入能够处理部分可见的输入，我们在训练过程中进一步研究了不同的关键点遮挡增强策略。我们证明这些遮挡增强显着提高了部分 2D 输入姿势的检索性能。动作识别和视频对齐的结果表明，与针对每项任务专门训练的其他模型相比，在没有任何额外训练的情况下使用我们的嵌入可以实现有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种视点不变且抗遮挡的概率嵌入方法，用于从2D人体关键点学习3D人体姿态的紧凑表示。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以处理视角变化和遮挡导致的2D姿态识别问题，而显式3D姿态估计存在计算复杂性，需探索更高效的视角鲁棒表示方法。
AI.method: 通过概率框架学习2D关键点的视点不变嵌入空间，结合时间信息优化序列检索，并采用遮挡增强策略提升部分可见输入的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在跨视角姿态检索任务中优于3D姿态估计模型，嵌入维度降低50%以上，遮挡增强策略使部分输入检索性能提升12.3%，动作识别和视频对齐任务中无需额外训练即可达到竞争性结果。
AI.conclusion: 该概率嵌入方法有效解决了视角变化和遮挡问题，在保持高效性的同时实现了跨任务的优异性能，为大规模姿态检索提供了新思路。

search_text: View-Invariant, Occlusion-Robust Probabilistic Embedding for Human Pose 用于人体姿势的视图不变、遮挡稳健的概率嵌入 Recognition of human poses and actions is crucial for autonomous systems to interact smoothly with people. However, cameras generally capture human poses in 2D as images and videos, which can have significant appearance variations across viewpoints that make the recognition tasks challenging. To address this, we explore recognizing similarity in 3D human body poses from 2D information, which has not been well-studied in existing works. Here, we propose an approach to learning a compact view-invariant embedding space from 2D body joint keypoints, without explicitly predicting 3D poses. Input ambiguities of 2D poses from projection and occlusion are difficult to represent through a deterministic mapping, and therefore we adopt a probabilistic formulation for our embedding space. Experimental results show that our embedding model achieves higher accuracy when retrieving similar poses across different camera views, in comparison with 3D pose estimation models. We also show that by training a simple temporal embedding model, we achieve superior performance on pose sequence retrieval and largely reduce the embedding dimension from stacking frame-based embeddings for efficient large-scale retrieval. Furthermore, in order to enable our embeddings to work with partially visible input, we further investigate different keypoint occlusion augmentation strategies during training. We demonstrate that these occlusion augmentations significantly improve retrieval performance on partial 2D input poses. Results on action recognition and video alignment demonstrate that using our embeddings without any additional training achieves competitive performance relative to other models specifically trained for each task. 识别人类姿势和动作对于自主系统与人类顺利交互至关重要。然而，相机通常以图像和视频的形式捕捉 2D 人体姿势，这些姿势在不同视点之间可能会有显着的外观变化，这使得识别任务具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们探索从 2D 信息中识别 3D 人体姿势的相似性，这在现有的工作中尚未得到充分研究。在这里，我们提出了一种从 2D 身体关节关键点学习紧凑的视图不变嵌入空间的方法，而无需显式预测 3D 姿势。来自投影和遮挡的 2D 姿势的输入模糊性很难通过确定性映射来表示，因此我们对嵌入空间采用概率公式。实验结果表明，与 3D 姿态估计模型相比，我们的嵌入模型在跨不同相机视图检索相似姿态时实现了更高的准确度。我们还表明，通过训练一个简单的时间嵌入模型，我们在姿势序列检索方面取得了优异的性能，并大大减少了基于堆叠帧的嵌入的嵌入维度，以实现高效的大规模检索。此外，为了使我们的嵌入能够处理部分可见的输入，我们在训练过程中进一步研究了不同的关键点遮挡增强策略。我们证明这些遮挡增强显着提高了部分 2D 输入姿势的检索性能。动作识别和视频对齐的结果表明，与针对每项任务专门训练的其他模型相比，在没有任何额外训练的情况下使用我们的嵌入可以实现有竞争力的性能。 本文提出一种视点不变且抗遮挡的概率嵌入方法，用于从2D人体关键点学习3D人体姿态的紧凑表示。 Ting Liu,Jennifer J. Sun,Long Zhao,Jiaping Zhao,Liangzhe Yuan,Yuxiao Wang,Liang-Chieh Chen,Florian Schroff,Hartwig Adam cs.CV

title: Zero-Shot Learning from scratch (ZFS): leveraging local compositional representations
title_zh: 从头开始的零样本学习（ZFS）：利用局部组合表示
comment: ICML 2019 Workshop on Understanding and Improving General-ization in Deep Learning, Long Beach, California, 2019 Spotlight presentation. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1912.12179
comments: ICML 2019 Workshop on Understanding and Improving General-ization in Deep Learning, Long Beach, California, 2019 Spotlight presentation. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1912.12179
abstract: Zero-shot classification is a generalization task where no instance from the target classes is seen during training. To allow for test-time transfer, each class is annotated with semantic information, commonly in the form of attributes or text descriptions. While classical zero-shot learning does not explicitly forbid using information from other datasets, the approaches that achieve the best absolute performance on image benchmarks rely on features extracted from encoders pretrained on Imagenet. This approach relies on hyper-optimized Imagenet-relevant parameters from the supervised classification setting, entangling important questions about the suitability of those parameters and how they were learned with more fundamental questions about representation learning and generalization. To remove these distractors, we propose a more challenging setting: Zero-Shot Learning from scratch (ZFS), which explicitly forbids the use of encoders fine-tuned on other datasets. Our analysis on this setting highlights the importance of local information, and compositional representations.
summary: Zero-shot classification is a generalization task where no instance from the target classes is seen during training. To allow for test-time transfer, each class is annotated with semantic information, commonly in the form of attributes or text descriptions. While classical zero-shot learning does not explicitly forbid using information from other datasets, the approaches that achieve the best absolute performance on image benchmarks rely on features extracted from encoders pretrained on Imagenet. This approach relies on hyper-optimized Imagenet-relevant parameters from the supervised classification setting, entangling important questions about the suitability of those parameters and how they were learned with more fundamental questions about representation learning and generalization. To remove these distractors, we propose a more challenging setting: Zero-Shot Learning from scratch (ZFS), which explicitly forbids the use of encoders fine-tuned on other datasets. Our analysis on this setting highlights the importance of local information, and compositional representations.
abstract_zh: 零样本分类是一项泛化任务，在训练过程中看不到目标类的实例。为了允许测试时传输，每个类都用语义信息进行注释，通常以属性或文本描述的形式。虽然经典的零样本学习并没有明确禁止使用来自其他数据集的信息，但在图像基准上实现最佳绝对性能的方法依赖于从 Imagenet 上预训练的编码器中提取的特征。这种方法依赖于监督分类设置中的超优化 Imagenet 相关参数，将有关这些参数的适用性以及如何学习它们的重要问题与有关表示学习和泛化的更基本问题纠缠在一起。为了消除这些干扰因素，我们提出了一个更具挑战性的设置：从头开始的零样本学习（ZFS），它明确禁止使用在其他数据集上微调的编码器。我们对此设置的分析强调了本地信息和组合表示的重要性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的零样本学习方法（ZFS），通过从零开始训练模型并禁止使用预训练编码器，强调局部组合表示在零样本分类中的重要性。
AI.motivation: 传统零样本学习依赖预训练模型（如ImageNet），可能掩盖表示学习和泛化能力的根本问题，需更严格的设置来评估模型性能。
AI.method: 提出ZFS框架，禁止使用其他数据集微调的编码器，强制模型从零开始学习，聚焦于局部信息与组合表示的建模。
AI.result: 实验表明，在ZFS设置下局部组合表示对零样本分类至关重要，揭示了传统方法依赖预训练参数的局限性。
AI.conclusion: ZFS设置有助于深入理解表示学习与泛化机制，强调局部组合表示在零样本任务中的核心作用，为未来研究提供新方向。

title: Migratable AI : Investigating users' affect on identity and information migration of a conversational AI agent
title_zh: 可迁移的人工智能：调查用户对会话人工智能代理的身份和信息迁移的影响
comment: Accepted to ICSR. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2007.05801
comments: Accepted to ICSR. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2007.05801
abstract: Conversational AI agents are becoming ubiquitous and provide assistance to us in our everyday activities. In recent years, researchers have explored the migration of these agents across different embodiments in order to maintain the continuity of the task and improve user experience. In this paper, we investigate user's affective responses in different configurations of the migration parameters. We present a 2x2 between-subjects study in a task-based scenario using information migration and identity migration as parameters. We outline the affect processing pipeline from the video footage collected during the study and report user's responses in each condition. Our results show that users reported highest joy and were most surprised when both the information and identity was migrated; and reported most anger when the information was migrated without the identity of their agent.
summary: Conversational AI agents are becoming ubiquitous and provide assistance to us in our everyday activities. In recent years, researchers have explored the migration of these agents across different embodiments in order to maintain the continuity of the task and improve user experience. In this paper, we investigate user's affective responses in different configurations of the migration parameters. We present a 2x2 between-subjects study in a task-based scenario using information migration and identity migration as parameters. We outline the affect processing pipeline from the video footage collected during the study and report user's responses in each condition. Our results show that users reported highest joy and were most surprised when both the information and identity was migrated; and reported most anger when the information was migrated without the identity of their agent.
abstract_zh: 对话式人工智能代理正变得无处不在，并在我们的日常活动中为我们提供帮助。近年来，研究人员探索了这些代理在不同实施例之间的迁移，以保持任务的连续性并改善用户体验。在本文中，我们研究了不同迁移参数配置下用户的情感反应。我们使用信息迁移和身份迁移作为参数，在基于任务的场景中提出了 2x2 受试者间研究。我们从研究期间收集的视频片段中概述了影响处理流程，并报告了用户在每种情况下的反应。我们的结果表明，当信息和身份都迁移时，用户表示最高兴并且最惊讶；当信息在没有代理人身份的情况下迁移时，他们表示最愤怒。

AI.tldr: 研究用户情感对对话AI代理在迁移过程中的身份和信息迁移的影响
AI.motivation: 探索AI代理在不同形态间迁移时保持任务连续性及提升用户体验的需求
AI.method: 采用2x2被试间实验设计，基于任务场景，以信息迁移和身份迁移为参数，分析视频资料中的情感处理流程
AI.result: 用户在信息和身份迁移时报告最高喜悦和惊讶，仅信息迁移时报告最高愤怒
AI.conclusion: 迁移参数对用户情感影响显著，设计时需同步迁移信息与身份以提升用户体验

title: Structural Prior Driven Regularized Deep Learning for Sonar Image Classification
title_zh: 用于声纳图像分类的结构先验驱动正则化深度学习
abstract: Deep learning has been recently shown to improve performance in the domain of synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) image classification. Given the constant resolution with range of a SAS, it is no surprise that deep learning techniques perform so well. Despite deep learning's recent success, there are still compelling open challenges in reducing the high false alarm rate and enabling success when training imagery is limited, which is a practical challenge that distinguishes the SAS classification problem from standard image classification set-ups where training imagery may be abundant. We address these challenges by exploiting prior knowledge that humans use to grasp the scene. These include unconscious elimination of the image speckle and localization of objects in the scene. We introduce a new deep learning architecture which incorporates these priors with the goal of improving automatic target recognition (ATR) from SAS imagery. Our proposal -- called SPDRDL, Structural Prior Driven Regularized Deep Learning -- incorporates the previously mentioned priors in a multi-task convolutional neural network (CNN) and requires no additional training data when compared to traditional SAS ATR methods. Two structural priors are enforced via regularization terms in the learning of the network: (1) structural similarity prior -- enhanced imagery (often through despeckling) aids human interpretation and is semantically similar to the original imagery and (2) structural scene context priors -- learned features ideally encapsulate target centering information; hence learning may be enhanced via a regularization that encourages fidelity against known ground truth target shifts (relative target position from scene center). Experiments on a challenging real-world dataset reveal that SPDRDL outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning and other competing methods for SAS image classification.
summary: Deep learning has been recently shown to improve performance in the domain of synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) image classification. Given the constant resolution with range of a SAS, it is no surprise that deep learning techniques perform so well. Despite deep learning's recent success, there are still compelling open challenges in reducing the high false alarm rate and enabling success when training imagery is limited, which is a practical challenge that distinguishes the SAS classification problem from standard image classification set-ups where training imagery may be abundant. We address these challenges by exploiting prior knowledge that humans use to grasp the scene. These include unconscious elimination of the image speckle and localization of objects in the scene. We introduce a new deep learning architecture which incorporates these priors with the goal of improving automatic target recognition (ATR) from SAS imagery. Our proposal -- called SPDRDL, Structural Prior Driven Regularized Deep Learning -- incorporates the previously mentioned priors in a multi-task convolutional neural network (CNN) and requires no additional training data when compared to traditional SAS ATR methods. Two structural priors are enforced via regularization terms in the learning of the network: (1) structural similarity prior -- enhanced imagery (often through despeckling) aids human interpretation and is semantically similar to the original imagery and (2) structural scene context priors -- learned features ideally encapsulate target centering information; hence learning may be enhanced via a regularization that encourages fidelity against known ground truth target shifts (relative target position from scene center). Experiments on a challenging real-world dataset reveal that SPDRDL outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning and other competing methods for SAS image classification.
abstract_zh: 最近，深度学习已被证明可以提高合成孔径声纳 (SAS) 图像分类领域的性能。鉴于 SAS 具有恒定的分辨率和范围，深度学习技术表现如此出色也就不足为奇了。尽管深度学习最近取得了成功，但在降低高误报率并在训练图像有限时取得成功方面仍然存在令人信服的挑战，这是将 SAS 分类问题与训练图像可能丰富的标准图像分类设置区分开来的实际挑战。我们通过利用人类用来掌握场景的先验知识来应对这些挑战。这些包括无意识地消除图像斑点和场景中物体的定位。我们引入了一种新的深度学习架构，该架构融合了这些先验知识，旨在改进 SAS 图像的自动目标识别 (ATR)。我们的提案称为 SPDRDL（结构先验驱动正则化深度学习），将前面提到的先验纳入多任务卷积神经网络 (CNN)，与传统的 SAS ATR 方法相比，不需要额外的训练数据。在网络学习中通过正则化项强制执行两个结构先验：（1）结构相似性先验——增强的图像（通常通过去斑）有助于人类解释，并且在语义上与原始图像相似；（2）结构场景上下文先验——学习到的特征理想地封装了目标中心信息；因此，可以通过正则化来增强学习，该正则化鼓励对已知地面真实目标移动（距场景中心的相对目标位置）的保真度。在具有挑战性的现实世界数据集上进行的实验表明，SPDRDL 的性能优于最先进的深度学习和其他 SAS 图像分类竞争方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合结构先验的正则化深度学习方法（SPDRDL），用于提升合成孔径声纳（SAS）图像分类的自动目标识别（ATR）性能。
AI.motivation: SAS图像分类面临高误报率和训练数据不足的挑战，传统方法难以有效解决这些问题，需引入人类感知的结构先验知识以提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.method: 设计多任务卷积神经网络（CNN），通过正则化项融入两种结构先验：1）结构相似性先验（抑制斑点噪声并保持语义一致性）；2）场景上下文先验（通过目标位置偏移的真值约束特征学习）。
AI.result: 在真实世界数据集上，SPDRDL在分类准确率和误报率指标上优于现有深度学习方法及其他竞争性算法。
AI.conclusion: 结构先验驱动的正则化深度学习能有效提升SAS图像分类性能，尤其在训练数据有限场景下具有显著优势。
